message:
fix(vdbe): infer table-vs-index B-tree kind from page headers

Previously, open_storage_cursor always assumed table B-trees
(is_table_btree=true), which caused index cursors to be opened with
the wrong B-tree kind. This led to incorrect cell parsing and seek
behavior for index roots.

Changes:
- Parse BtreePageHeader on existing root pages to detect the actual
  page type (LeafTable, InteriorTable, LeafIndex, InteriorIndex) and
  pass the correct is_table_btree flag to BtCursor::new.
- For zero-page initialization of writable cursors, consult
  MemDatabase to determine whether the root is a table or index,
  then initialize with the appropriate leaf page type byte (0x0D
  for LeafTable, 0x0A for LeafIndex).
- For the MemPageStore fallback path, similarly consult MemDatabase
  to create with_empty_table vs with_empty_index and only populate
  from MemDatabase rows when the root is a table.
- Add detected_page_type, initialized_page_type, and is_table_btree
  fields to all tracing::debug spans for cursor open decisions.
- Import BtreePageHeader, BtreePageType, and header_offset_for_page
  from fsqlite_btree.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
